topic: EXP-462 P1 canonical-hub pointer above §6.14 κ h3 (verification)
change_summary:
Decider c581944 integrated EXP-462 P1 — inserted one

pointer between

and the existing '

What κ is and why the dome cannot abandon it

' in §6.14. Body content unchanged; net +750 bytes in part6.html (140187→140937).
our_argument_summary: §6.14 now opens with an unanchored scaffolding paragraph clarifying that this section's contribution is the cross-event κ-vise (Mohe 1997 κ extrapolated to 1999 European / 2009 East Asian eclipses where SG gravimeters saw zero) plus the BOU / W004 / monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity audit, and that the magnitude-only Pass-band non-discrimination argument lives at §4.2.5. The pointer is plain prose, no <strong> emphasis, and reads as orientation not as a new claim.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: N/A — this is a navigation-aid prose patch to our review, not a WIN review of dome code.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — the pointer makes no code-level claims; it only references downstream §6.14 content (BOU / W004 / monitor.py-KAPPA-squared heterogeneity) that is itself reviewed elsewhere (SEC-6.14-kappa-cluster.c7 and predecessors).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: N/A — this is a prose patch to a section navigation pointer, not a WIN-level monitoring claim.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: N/A — pointer adds no claims about phenomena; just routes the reader.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: N/A — pointer is a structural navigation aid, not a prediction or observation claim.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: N/A — pointer makes no derivation; it references §4.2.5 where the derivation-vs-adoption critique already lives.

holes_found.description: Capitalization of 'Pass band' in the inserted pointer ('the magnitude-only Pass band non-discrimination argument') reads as if 'Pass band' is a defined term, but neither §4.2.5 nor §4.2.6 names it that way. The capitalization is most plausibly drawn from the dome's monitor.py 'pass' status convention, but a reader following the link from §6.14 will not find 'Pass band' as a named concept at the destination. Recommend lowercasing to 'pass-band' or rephrasing to 'magnitude-only band-overlap non-discrimination argument' so the term in §6.14 matches what they will encounter at §4.2.5.
holes_found.recommendation: In sections.json part6.html, change 'magnitude-only Pass band non-discrimination argument' to 'magnitude-only pass-band non-discrimination argument' (lowercase 'p', hyphenated) so it reads as plain descriptive prose rather than a named term.

holes_found.description: Terminology drift between pointer and destination. The pointer characterizes the §4.2.5 decomposition as '(baseline × coverage × geometry weight)' but §4.2.5 itself writes ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, where FSF is explicitly named the Footprint-Scaling Factor. 'Geometry weight' is an acceptable plain-English gloss of FSF, but a reader following the cross-reference will not see 'geometry weight' at the destination; they will see 'FSF'. Marginal — the gloss is fair — but a precise reader could notice the inconsistency.
holes_found.recommendation: Either change the pointer to '(baseline × coverage × FSF)' to match §4.2.5's actual term, or accept the gloss and add a parenthetical in §4.2.5 such as '(FSF, the geometry weight)'. The former is the smaller edit.

stronger_arguments.description: The pointer's framing — 'this section's contribution is X; the Y argument lives at §4.2.5; this section assumes that argument and proceeds' — is exactly the right shape for a canonical-hub scaffold. EXP-462's design goal (kill the 39-fold Chapman re-derivation across §§4/5/6/8/8b and route everything through §4.2 as the hub) is structurally served by this kind of disclaimer-at-the-top. Encourage analyst to apply the same pattern to §6.7 (the other §6 deferred-target the EXP-462 target list mentioned) and to §§8/8b on the next pass.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-462 P1 correctly identifies the structural redundancy problem in §6.14 — without the pointer, a reader who lands at §6.14 cannot tell whether the 'baseline × coverage × X' decomposition there is meant to ADD to the §4.2.5 argument or RECAPITULATE it (data/sections.json:part6.html §6.14 prior state). The decomposition appears in §4.2.5 (ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF), in §4.2.4 (Chapman arithmetic comparison), and again implicitly in §6.14's discussion of W004 recalibration math — and HOLISTIC-HOL-REDUNDANCY.c2's 39-instance Chapman re-derivation count confirms the cross-section duplication is a real review-side problem, not a defender talking point (monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/HOLISTIC-HOL-REDUNDANCY.c2.json). A single scaffold sentence at the top of §6.14 is the minimum effective intervention — anything larger risks re-deriving the §4.2 argument, which is exactly what the redundancy hole flagged.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: There is no claim here to save — the pointer adds no thesis. Its job is to reduce reader confusion about §6.14's unique contribution vs §4.2.5's. The pointer succeeds on that job; the two minor nits above (Pass-band capitalization, geometry-weight vs FSF gloss) do not undermine the navigation function. The §6.14 substantive content (κ-vise, dual-formula audit, 1999/2009 SG null) is preserved verbatim, so the underlying argumentative content of part6.html remains unaffected by this integration. Verification-class verdict therefore holds: integration is clean; the two minor nits should route to a small follow-up patch rather than blocking the patch as integrated.

text_fingerprint.verdict: n/a (section-rewrite verification — no per-section verdict field)

deeper_analysis:
Verification-class checks: (1) Insertion point matches EXP-462 P1 spec — '

This section\'s contribution...' lands between the closing '' and '

' wrapper and the existing '

What κ is and why the dome cannot abandon it

'. Confirmed by inspection of git diff c581944^..c581944 on data/sections.json (the net +750-byte block is exactly the inserted

). (2) Anchor #p4-425 is a valid destination — found in data/sections.json:part4 at byte offset 59076, rendering '4.2.5 Structural problems'. The destination's content (no dome geometry in formula, quiet-condition filtering, third attempt with widening criteria) IS the magnitude-only non-discrimination argument the pointer claims lives there, anchored by §4.2.5 Point 1 (ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF). (3) No existing §6.14 content disturbed — TLDR, h3, kappa derivation paragraph all verbatim before and after. (4) docs/index.html rebuilt — the inserted paragraph appears at line 4152 with identical wording. (5) The pointer is unambiguous as scaffolding: 'this section assumes that argument and proceeds' tells the reader explicitly that §6.14 is downstream of §4.2.5, not parallel to it. That sentence-level discipline is what the analyst was asked to deliver and is what is on the page. The two minor nits (Pass-band capitalization, geometry-weight terminology) are stylistic and can be batched with the next §6.14 pass.

summary_for_decider:
EXP-462 P1 integration is clean. Pointer is scaffolding-not-thesis (confirmed: plain

wrapper, no , 'this section assumes that argument and proceeds' framing). #p4-425 link valid. No existing content disturbed (TLDR, h3, kappa derivation verbatim). Two minor stylistic nits worth a small follow-up patch: (a) 'Pass band' capitalization implies a named term that doesn't exist at the destination — recommend lowercase 'pass-band'; (b) 'geometry weight' in the pointer vs 'FSF' at §4.2.5 — recommend aligning terminology. Neither nit blocks the integration. Verification-class verdict: PASS. PROP-009: queue_id 430, matches queue item pushed 2026-05-31T17:11:36.453Z.
